name: Saline Nasal Spray
note: Isotonic or hypertonic saline. Safe for all ages including infants and during pregnancy. Moisturizes nasal passages and clears allergens and debris.

name: Nasya Oil
note: Ayurvedic sesame or herbal oil applied into the nostrils. Lubricates and protects nasal passages. Traditional morning practice; particularly useful in dry climates.

name: Neti Pot
note: Nasal irrigation device. Use sterile or distilled water only — never tap water. Effectively clears allergens, mucus, and environmental irritants.

name: Fluticasone Nasal Spray
note: OTC intranasal corticosteroid. First-line treatment for seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis. Requires 1-2 weeks for full anti-inflammatory effect. Minimal systemic absorption.

name: Budesonide Nasal Spray
note: OTC intranasal corticosteroid. Alternative to fluticasone for allergic rhinitis. Effective for reducing nasal inflammation, congestion, and post-nasal drip.

name: Olopatadine Nasal Spray
note: Prescription antihistamine nasal spray for allergic rhinitis. Provides rapid symptom relief of nasal itching, sneezing, and congestion.

name: Xylitol Nasal Spray
note: Xylitol-based nasal spray that may inhibit bacterial adhesion to nasal mucosa. Moisturizing and antimicrobial. Safe for daily use and during pregnancy.

name: Bioidentical Progesterone Cream
note: Over-the-counter or compounded topical progesterone cream. Used for PMS, perimenopause, and luteal phase support. Apply to thin-skinned areas (inner wrist, inner thigh) and rotate sites.

name: Topical Magnesium
note: Magnesium chloride applied topically for potential transdermal absorption. Used for muscle cramps, soreness, and sleep support. May cause mild tingling on sensitive skin.

name: Castor Oil
note: Thick plant oil with anti-inflammatory and lymphatic-stimulating properties. Castor oil packs applied to abdomen support liver detoxification, reduce inflammation, and improve gut motility. Also used for hair growth.

name: Arnica Gel
note: Topical homeopathic arnica for bruising, muscle soreness, and post-injury swelling. Apply to unbroken skin only. Available in gel and cream forms.

name: Zinc Oxide Cream
note: Physical skin barrier cream with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Used for diaper rash, minor irritations, wound protection, and as a sunscreen active ingredient.

name: CBD Topical Cream
note: Topical hemp-derived CBD cream for localized pain, inflammation, and muscle tension. Minimal systemic absorption. Look for third-party tested products with confirmed CBD content.

name: DMSO Gel
note: Pharmaceutical-grade dimethyl sulfoxide. Penetrates skin deeply and carries substances into tissue. Used for localized pain, inflammation, and tendon injuries. Use pharmaceutical grade only.

name: Non-Toxic Deodorant
note: Deodorant free of aluminum, parabens, artificial fragrance, and phthalates. Common active ingredients include magnesium, baking soda, arrowroot, and essential oils. May require a transition period.

name: Fluoride-Free Toothpaste
note: Toothpaste without fluoride, SLS, artificial sweeteners, or synthetic dyes. Options include hydroxyapatite toothpaste (remineralizing), charcoal, or herbal formulations. Hydroxyapatite is evidence-based.

name: Tongue Scraper
note: Remove tongue coating each morning before eating or drinking. Reduces oral bacteria, sulfur compounds, and toxins. Copper scrapers preferred in Ayurvedic tradition for antimicrobial properties.

name: Water Flosser
note: Pressurized water stream for interdental cleaning and gum pocket irrigation. More effective than string floss for implants, braces, and deep gum pockets. Complements but does not fully replace flossing.

name: Mineral Sunscreen
note: Mineral (physical) sunscreen using zinc oxide and/or titanium dioxide. Sits on skin surface rather than being absorbed. Preferred over chemical sunscreens for daily use and in pregnancy.

name: Non-Toxic Shampoo
note: Shampoo free of sulfates, parabens, synthetic fragrance, and silicones. Look for EWG Verified or third-party clean formulations. Scalp health benefits from avoiding harsh detergents.

name: Red Light Therapy Device
note: At-home or clinical device emitting red (630-700nm) and near-infrared (800-850nm) wavelengths. Used for skin rejuvenation, hair growth, wound healing, pain, and mitochondrial support. 10-20 minutes per session.

name: PEMF Mat
note: Delivers pulsed electromagnetic fields to stimulate cellular repair and circulation. Used for pain, inflammation, bone healing, and sleep improvement. FDA-cleared devices available for home use.

name: Infrared Sauna Blanket
note: Portable far-infrared blanket for sweat sessions at home. Promotes detoxification, muscle recovery, and relaxation. More accessible than full sauna. Session length 30-45 minutes.

name: Cold Plunge Tub
note: Dedicated cold water immersion vessel for home use. Maintains consistent water temperature (45-59°F). Used for post-workout recovery, inflammation reduction, and mental resilience.

name: Grounding Mat
note: Conductive mat connected to earth ground via electrical outlet grounding pin. Used for indoor grounding during sleep or desk work. Proposed to reduce inflammation and improve sleep.

name: TENS Unit
note: Delivers mild electrical impulses to modulate pain signals. Used for muscle pain, joint pain, menstrual cramps, and nerve pain. FDA-cleared OTC units available for home use.

name: Massage Gun
note: Handheld percussive device for muscle recovery, tension release, and pre/post-workout use. Use on muscle belly — avoid joints, spine, and inflamed tissue.

name: Foam Roller
note: Self-myofascial release tool for reducing muscle tension, improving mobility, and post-workout recovery. Use slowly on major muscle groups. Avoid rolling directly on joints or the lumbar spine.

name: Acupressure Mat
note: Mat with thousands of plastic pressure points applied to back, neck, and feet for self-acupressure. Used for relaxation, back pain, improved sleep, and endorphin release.

name: HEPA Air Purifier
note: High-efficiency particulate air filtration device removing 99.97% of particles 0.3 microns or larger. Essential for reducing indoor air allergens, mold spores, dust, and particulates. Run continuously in bedroom.

name: Blue Light Glasses
note: Lenses filtering blue light wavelengths (380-500nm). Used in evening to reduce melatonin suppression from screens and artificial light. Amber-tinted lenses provide stronger protection than clear.

name: Continuous Glucose Monitor
note: Wearable sensor providing real-time interstitial glucose data. Used by diabetics and non-diabetics for metabolic health optimization. Illuminates food, sleep, stress, and exercise effects on blood sugar.

name: Prenatal Vitamin
note: Comprehensive prenatal supplement. Prioritize formulations containing L-methylfolate (not just folic acid), methylcobalamin, choline, iodine, iron, and DHA. Begin pre-conception when possible.

name: Ginger Chews
note: Ginger-containing chews or candies for nausea relief in pregnancy. Ginger is category A-safe and evidence-supported for morning sickness. Choose low-sugar formulations.

name: Doxylamine + Pyridoxine
note: First-line FDA-approved treatment for nausea and vomiting of pregnancy. Begin with bedtime dose; titrate based on symptom pattern. Available as prescription combination or OTC components.

name: Docusate Sodium
note: Stool softener safe during pregnancy and postpartum. Draws water into stool to ease passage. Does not stimulate contractions. Useful in third trimester and post-delivery.

name: Calcium Carbonate Antacid
note: Fast-acting antacid for heartburn and reflux in pregnancy. Safe in all trimesters. Also provides supplemental calcium. Common in third trimester as growing uterus displaces stomach.

name: Belly Butter
note: Rich emollient applied to abdomen, breasts, and hips during pregnancy. Keeps skin supple and may reduce discomfort from stretching. Choose fragrance-free, mineral oil-free formulations.

name: Nipple Cream
note: Purified lanolin or plant-based balm for sore and cracked nipples during breastfeeding. Safe for infant — no need to wipe off before nursing. Proper latch assessment is the primary fix for soreness.

name: Perineal Massage Oil
note: Gentle oil (vitamin E, sweet almond, or coconut) for perineal massage beginning at 34-36 weeks. Evidence supports reducing severe perineal tearing in first-time vaginal births.

name: Postnatal Vitamin
note: Optimized for postpartum recovery and lactation. Higher choline, iodine, and DHA than standard multivitamins. Continue for duration of breastfeeding and at least 3-6 months postpartum.

name: Witch Hazel Pads
note: Cooling witch hazel pads for postpartum perineal care. Reduces swelling, hemorrhoids, and discomfort. Place in pad or apply directly to perineum. Use alcohol-free formulations.

name: Sitz Bath Kit
note: Shallow warm water bath for perineum following vaginal delivery or hemorrhoid relief. 15-20 minutes, 2-3 times daily. Can add Epsom salts or herbal rinse. Over-toilet basin or bathtub.

name: Perineal Spray
note: Cooling anesthetic or herbal spray for postpartum perineal discomfort. Benzocaine-containing sprays (Dermoplast) provide topical numbing. Witch hazel and aloe vera sprays are herbal alternatives.

name: Lactation Support Supplement
note: Herbal blend containing fenugreek, blessed thistle, fennel, or moringa to support breast milk production. Work best alongside frequent nursing or pumping and adequate nutrition and hydration.

name: Postpartum Recovery Belt
note: Supportive abdominal wrap worn postpartum to support core, reduce discomfort, and improve comfort during recovery. Use for 4-8 weeks. Not appropriate during active diastasis recti rehabilitation without guidance.

name: Placenta Encapsulation
note: Dehydrated and encapsulated placenta for postpartum consumption. Anecdotally used to reduce postpartum mood symptoms and fatigue. Limited clinical evidence. Ensure proper preparation and handling by trained specialist.

name: Tretinoin Cream
note: Topical retinoid (vitamin A acid) for acne, photoaging, and fine lines. FDA-approved for acne; highly evidence-based for anti-aging. Start low (0.025%), apply at night. Avoid in pregnancy.

name: Azelaic Acid
note: Topical dicarboxylic acid for acne, rosacea, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, and melasma. Pregnancy-safe. Available OTC (10%) and prescription (15-20%). Suitable for sensitive skin.

name: Benzoyl Peroxide Wash
note: 2.5-10% benzoyl peroxide cleanser for acne on face and body. Kills P. acnes bacteria and reduces follicular plugging. Wash off formulations cause less dryness than leave-on. Bleaches fabric.

name: Salicylic Acid Cleanser
note: Beta-hydroxy acid (BHA) face wash that exfoliates inside pores and reduces sebum buildup. Effective for blackheads, congestion, and mild-to-moderate acne. Concentrations of 0.5-2% are most common.
